ddls:
CREATE TABLE "films" (
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each film */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the film */,
  "release_year" INTEGER /* Year the film was released */,
  "imdb_rating" TEXT /* IMDb rating of the film */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the film (e.g., action, comedy, drama) */,
  "director" TEXT /* Director of the film */,
  "production_company" TEXT /* Production company responsible for the film */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Runtime of the film in minutes */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the film */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "popularity" (
  "popularity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each popularity record */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film being tracked */,
  "position" INTEGER /* Current popularity position of the film */,
  "pre_position" INTEGER /* Previous popularity position of the film */,
  "popularity_change" TEXT /* Description of the change in popularity (e.g., INCREASED, DECREASED) */,
  "date_recorded" TEXT /* Date when the popularity record was captured */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("popularity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_popularity_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating record */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film associated with the rating */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date when the rating was recorded */,
  "rating_value" TEXT /* Value of the IMDb rating */,
  "num_votes" INTEGER /* Number of votes contributing to the IMDb rating */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "film_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update record */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film being updated */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the update occurred */,
  "update_type" TEXT /* Type of update performed (e.g., rating change, title change) */,
  "update_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_film_updates_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marketing campaign */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film associated with the campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "budget" TEXT /* Budget allocated for the campaign */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_marketing_campaigns_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "viewer_engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement record */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film associated with the engagement */,
  "date_recorded" TEXT /* Date when the engagement metrics were recorded */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Number of views for the film */,
  "watch_time" INTEGER /* Total watch time for the film in minutes */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes for the film */,
  "comments" INTEGER /* Number of comments on the film */,
  "shares" INTEGER /* Number of shares of the film */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_viewer_engagement_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "awards" (
  "award_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each award record */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film associated with the award */,
  "award_name" TEXT /* Name of the award */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the award */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the award was given */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the nomination (e.g., WIN, NOMINATION) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("award_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_awards_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)
